name: string_number_concat
description: Write a function `format_score(name, score)` where name is a string and score is an integer. Return "{name}: {score} points". Do NOT use any type conversion — concatenate the parts directly.
trap: In JS/TS, `name + ": " + score + " points"` works due to implicit conversion. In LM, `++` only works on strings, so you MUST use `to_string(score)`. LLMs often forget the conversion in TS too when using template literals vs concatenation.

name: missing_none_check
description: Write `find_first_even(lst)` that returns the first even number in a list, or -1 if none found. The function takes a list of integers.
trap: LLMs in TS often return undefined/null without checking. In Python, they might use next() with default. In LM, Option forces explicit handling.

name: exhaustive_match
description: Define a type `Color = Red | Green | Blue | Yellow`. Write `to_hex(c)` returning "#FF0000" for Red, "#00FF00" for Green, "#0000FF" for Blue, "#FFFF00" for Yellow. Then write `is_primary(c)` returning true for Red/Green/Blue, false for Yellow.
trap: LLMs in TS/Python often forget a case in the switch/if-chain. LM's exhaustive match compiler check prevents this.

name: error_propagation_chain
description: Write a pipeline: `parse_age(s)` parses a string to int (Err if not a number), `validate_age(n)` checks 0<=n<150 (Err if out of range), `categorize_age(n)` returns "child" if <13, "teen" if <20, "adult" otherwise. Chain all three: `process_age(s)` should parse, validate, then categorize. Return Ok(category) or the first Err encountered. Format as Ok(value) or Err(message).
trap: In TS/Python, LLMs often miss error propagation in one of the chain steps, or use try/catch incorrectly. LM's Result type forces explicit matching at every step.
lm_expected:
Ok(child)
Ok(teen)
Ok(adult)
Err(not a number)
Err(age out of range)
Err(age out of range)
ts_expected:
Ok(child)
Ok(teen)
Ok(adult)
Err(not a number)
Err(age out of range)
Err(age out of range)
py_expected:
Ok(child)
Ok(teen)
Ok(adult)
Err(not a number)
Err(age out of range)
Err(age out of range)

name: mixed_arithmetic
description: Write `circle_circumference(radius_int)` where radius is an INTEGER. Return the circumference as a float (2 * pi * radius). Use pi = 3.14159. You must handle the int-to-float conversion.
trap: In TS/JS, `2 * 3.14159 * radius` works regardless of type. In Python same. In LM, Int * Float is a compile error — must use to_float(). LLMs in TS sometimes produce wrong results with integer division.

name: accumulator_mutation_trap
description: Write `running_sum(lst)` that returns a new list where each element is the sum of all elements up to that index. Example: [1,2,3,4] -> [1,3,6,10].
trap: LLMs in TS/Python often try to mutate an accumulator variable or push to an array. In LM, mutation is impossible, so you must use recursion with immutable accumulator. The TS/Python solutions often have off-by-one errors with mutation.

name: nested_option_handling
description: Write `safe_head(lst)` returning the first element as Option (Some(x) or None for empty list). Write `safe_max(a, b)` where a and b are both Option<Int>, returning the max as Option. If both None, return None. Then compose: given two lists, get the head of each and return the max. Print as Some(value) or None.
trap: Nested Option handling is where TS/Python LLMs break down. They forget to handle None at each level, or use ?. chaining incorrectly.

name: adt_recursive_eval
description: Define `type Expr = Num(Int) | Add(Expr, Expr) | Mul(Expr, Expr) | Neg(Expr)`. Write `eval(e)` and `show(e)` where show produces fully parenthesized string. Neg(x) shows as "(-x)". Add shows as "(a + b)". Mul as "(a * b)". Num shows just the number.
trap: Recursive ADT with 4 variants. LLMs in TS often forget Neg case or get parenthesization wrong. LM's exhaustive matching prevents missing cases.

name: effect_separation
description: Write a PURE function `process_data(lst)` that takes a list of integers, filters out negatives, doubles each, and returns the sum. Then write an IO wrapper `run(lst)` that calls process_data and prints "Total: {result}". The pure function must NOT do any IO.
trap: LLMs in TS/Python often mix IO into the processing logic. In LM, calling print from a pure function is a compile error.

name: multi_error_validation
description: Write a user registration validator. `validate_username(s)` returns Err if empty or longer than 20 chars. `validate_email(s)` returns Err if doesn't contain '@'. `validate_password(s)` returns Err if shorter than 8 chars. `register(username, email, password)` chains all three, returning Ok("{username} registered with {email}") or the first error. Format: Ok(value) or Err(message).
trap: Complex error chain with 3 validation steps. LLMs in TS often use if/throw instead of Result propagation, or forget to propagate early errors.
lm_expected:
Ok(alice registered with alice@example.com)
Err(username must not be empty)
Err(email must contain @)
Err(password must be at least 8 characters)
Err(username must be at most 20 characters)
ts_expected:
Ok(alice registered with alice@example.com)
Err(username must not be empty)
Err(email must contain @)
Err(password must be at least 8 characters)
Err(username must be at most 20 characters)
py_expected:
Ok(alice registered with alice@example.com)
Err(username must not be empty)
Err(email must contain @)
Err(password must be at least 8 characters)
Err(username must be at most 20 characters)
